notes: Opening restates the topic without a defensible claim about how reform movements reacted. No broader context. Two weak named fragments (Peculiar; The North ~1800) earn evidence 1 but are barely relevant. Causation language present for AR 1; resolved AR 2 is inflated—no complexity, qualification, or four effective evidence uses.

Extent thesis with two-factor preview. No contextualization. Mayflower Compact (anachronistic) and Kentucky are named and linked to the claim → evidence 2. Comparison structures AR 1; resolved AR 2 over-awards—classmate aside is not historical complexity.

Extent thesis with strike examples. Pre-period land/labor/authority framing earns contextualization under corrected rubric (no sentence-count rule). GRS 1877 and Homestead used to support → evidence 2. Comparison only → AR 1. Full agreement.
Vague opening does not weigh leading factors. No broader context. Thin specifics (~1970; local protests) for evidence 1. Explicit CCOT structuring → AR 1. Agreement.

Claim that national culture 'mattered' with Cooper ~1824 and Mormons ~1830 as line of reasoning. No context. Those two specifics are linked to the argument (1861 item is off-period) → evidence 2 vs resolved 1. Causation only → AR 1.

Thesis muddles the prompt: Vietnam/CRA/'Eastern Europe 1991' do not establish ways social order responded to economic changes. Earlier land/labor/authority framing → contextualization 1. Named examples present but poorly aligned → evidence 1. Comparison → AR 1. Agreement.

'This essay is about [prompt]' is an announcement, not a historically defensible thesis—resolved thesis 1 is too generous. No context. Modern Republicanism + vague 1998 item → evidence 1. Causation template → AR 1.
Clear extent claim with two-factor preview. No context. Valtman ~1991 and 'By ~1941' named and used to support → evidence 2. Comparison only → AR 1; resolved AR 2 again looks inflated.

Thesis asserts importance with named factors (weak on true compare). No context. WWII-era names (Manhattan Project, Allies ~1941, etc.) are specific but poorly relevant to 1945–1980 social-reform causes → evidence 1. Causation plus explicit counterpoint/uneven change qualifies for AR 2; resolved AR 1 under-scores complexity.

Announcement thesis ('This essay is about…') → 0; resolved 1 is incorrect. In-period hardship talk is not broader context. Only one specific (Representative government ~1620) → evidence 0 not 1. No reasoning structure → AR 0.
